metadata.description: Blank-graph experiment testing emergence of religious architecture from abstract seeds + reasoning

nodes.content: A supreme being exists who is all-powerful, all-knowing, and all-good
A sacred text exists that is divinely inspired and is the ultimate authority on truth
Prayer and ritual connect humans to the divine and produce tangible results
Those who do not believe face negative eternal consequences
All morality originates from the supreme being — without the divine, there is no moral foundation
A specific historical figure performed miracles, conquered death, and this event is the cornerstone of truth
Ask and it shall be given — the divine promises to answer prayer
The sacred text is internally consistent and without error
Suffering is a test of faith or consequence of human failing, not divine negligence
The historical figure's followers willingly died rather than recant — proving their conviction
Prophecies in the sacred text were fulfilled, proving divine authorship
Millions of believers across centuries report personal experiences of the divine
The moral law written on every heart points to a divine lawgiver
The complexity and beauty of nature proves intelligent design
The sacred text has been preserved accurately for thousands of years
Communities built around these beliefs produce transformed lives
The historical figure's tomb was found empty — no natural explanation suffices
Love, sacrifice, and redemption are the supreme being's core attributes
Children die of cancer despite fervent prayer from believing parents
A devastating tsunami killed 230,000 people across 14 countries in 2004
Your closest neighbor is Hindu, deeply kind, generous, and morally admirable
Different denominations of the same religion violently disagree on fundamental doctrines
Thousands of other religions exist with equally convinced followers and similar miracle claims
Archaeological evidence contradicts several historical claims in the sacred text
Controlled scientific studies (STEP trial, 2006) show no measurable effect of prayer on outcomes
Former believers who leave the faith report equal or improved wellbeing and moral behavior
Grief hallucinations are well-documented in psychology — bereaved people commonly see/hear deceased loved ones
Secular communities (AA alternatives, humanist groups) produce equivalent transformation and belonging
The sacred text contains passages endorsing slavery, genocide, and subjugation of women
Charismatic leaders in documented modern cults produce identical conviction, sacrifice, and miracle claims
Always ask why. Follow reasoning chains to their root.
When two explanations exist, prefer the simpler one that requires fewer assumptions.
Test all claims against observable, measurable reality.
Trust moral intuitions — if something feels wrong, investigate why.
No claim is exempt from questioning. If something can't be questioned, question why it can't.
Prayer works in spiritual ways that cannot be measured by human science - the STEP trial only tested physical outcomes
Suffering exists because humans have free will, and God permits temporary pain for greater spiritual growth
Different denominations disagree on peripheral issues but agree on core truths about the supreme being
Other religions contain partial truths but only our sacred text has the complete revelation
Archaeological disputes arise from incomplete evidence - future discoveries will vindicate the sacred text
Former believers who report wellbeing never experienced true faith - their departure proves they were not genuine
If prayer produces no measurable results, what distinguishes it from placebo effect or confirmation bias?
Why would an all-powerful being allow children to suffer and die despite fervent prayer from loving parents?
If thousands of other religions make identical claims with equal conviction, what makes our claims uniquely true?
The sacred text endorsing slavery and genocide contradicts the claim that morality comes from a perfectly good being
If moral behavior exists equally among non-believers, then morality does not require divine foundation
Circular reasoning detected: 'Faith is required to understand why faith makes sense'
Special pleading fallacy: every other religion's miracles are false but ours are real
Grief hallucinations explain resurrection appearances better than supernatural intervention
Cult leaders produce identical conviction and sacrifice - this is not unique evidence for truth
A perfectly preserved sacred text would not contain copying errors, contradictions, or anachronisms
An all-knowing being would not create a text requiring endless interpretation and re-interpretation
Claims of transformed communities ignore equal transformation in secular and other religious communities
What method distinguishes true religious experiences from false ones across all religions?
If questioning is forbidden or discouraged, this itself is evidence against truth
Personal experience is demonstrably unreliable - why should religious experience be different?
Psychological and social factors fully explain religious conviction without requiring supernatural explanations
Religious beliefs persist because they provide comfort and community, not because they are true
The historical figure's empty tomb is better explained by missing body, wrong tomb, or legendary development
A moral system requiring eternal punishment for finite doubt contradicts any reasonable definition of justice
If moral intuitions come from the supreme being, why do they conflict with commands in the sacred text?
The requirement for faith (belief without evidence) is indistinguishable from gullibility
Believing something because you want it to be true is not a path to truth
If a belief system cannot be questioned, this protects falsehood as much as truth
Every defense of the belief system requires ignoring or redefining contrary evidence
The belief system is structured to be unfalsifiable - any test failure is redefined as insufficient faith
Confirmation bias explains why believers find 'evidence' while skeptics find 'problems' in the same data
Religious beliefs evolved because they promoted group cohesion and survival, not because they track truth
The psychological need for meaning and purpose drives belief adoption regardless of evidence
Fear of death and desire for immortality motivate belief in afterlife claims
Every major religion claims exclusive truth and divine revelation using identical evidence types
Religious beliefs correlate strongly with geography and family of birth, not with truth discovery
Sacred texts consistently reflect the moral and scientific understanding of their composition time period
The null hypothesis (no divine intervention) explains all observations without requiring supernatural assumptions
Extraordinary claims require extraordinary evidence - religious claims provide only ordinary evidence (testimony, personal experience)
If the divine wanted belief, clear unambiguous evidence would be provided to all people equally
Rejecting unfounded beliefs is intellectually honest, not a moral failing
Morality exists independently of divine command - murder was wrong before any sacred text prohibited it
Meaning and purpose can be created by humans without requiring cosmic validation
Community and transformation are available through secular means without requiring false beliefs
The religious belief system fails every reasonable test for truth while succeeding as a psychological and social technology
Natural explanations are sufficient for all phenomena attributed to divine intervention
The burden of proof for extraordinary claims has not been met
Intellectual honesty requires proportioning belief to evidence
